message:
fix(models,bd-odbnh): thread manifest through check_model_installed so snowflake/nomic see correct file list

Pre-fix: src/search/model_download.rs:1760 `check_model_installed`
hardcoded `ModelManifest::minilm_v2()` to enumerate the files it
checked. After the v3of1 CLI fix (e66fa946) wired
`cass models install --model snowflake-arctic-s` and `... --model
nomic-embed` to their own model dirs, this function still checked
for MINILM's filenames in those dirs — so it always returned
NotInstalled regardless of on-disk state. The v3of1 commit message
explicitly flagged it as out-of-scope ("Worst-case UX is 'install
confirms download for already-installed snowflake/nomic'") but no
follow-up bead was filed until now (odbnh).

Fix: change the signature to
`check_model_installed(model_dir: &Path, manifest: &ModelManifest)`
and have callers pass the manifest they already resolved via
`ModelManifest::for_embedder(name)`.

Call sites updated:
- src/lib.rs:27491 (run_models_install) — already had `manifest` in
  scope from the v3of1 resolve pipeline.
- src/lib.rs:28180 (run_models_verify) — already had `manifest` in
  scope.
- src/search/model_download.rs test sites (3 tests) — all use
  ModelManifest::minilm_v2() explicitly.

Correctness: no behavioral change for the minilm path (the two
production callers currently resolve the same manifest they passed
implicitly via the hardcoded constant). For snowflake-arctic-s and
nomic-embed, the "already installed" short-circuit now fires when
the model is actually on disk, so `cass models install` no longer
re-triggers the downloader pipeline unnecessarily and
`cass models status` reports the correct `installed: true`.

Validation: cargo check --all-targets clean; 3/3
test_check_model_installed_* tests pass.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-odbnh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
